facts.text: In 1881, a railroad shortcut called the Bergen County Railroad opened, and a station was named Garfield after President James A. Garfield.
Garfield was incorporated as a borough on March 15, 1898, carved from parts of Saddle River Township and Wallington.
The area that is now Garfield was originally the homeland of the Hackensack Clan of the Leni Lenape people, who lived there thousands of years ago.
NJ Transit’s Bergen County Line stops at both Garfield and Plauderville stations, offering a direct ride to Hoboken Terminal and connections to Penn Station via Secaucus Junction.
A historical marker commemorates the Garfield YMCA Building at 33 Outwater Lane, noting its role in the community’s social history.
